title: The Frugendorff: Recursive Weight Sharing for Transformer Compression (1.1478 BPB, 15.19MB)
author: newjordan
quantization: int6 per-row with GPTQ Hessian-aware quantization

data.method: int6 per-row with GPTQ Hessian-aware quantization

data.description: K unique transformer blocks applied N times in sequence to produce deeper effective networks from fewer stored parameters
4 unique flat blocks run once, then 2 shared crawler blocks run twice with orthogonal positions to isolate gradient conflict
Learned consensus parameter with bidirectional gradient flow between recursive firings to improve communication and model quality
4x MLP expansion enabled by parameter savings from weight sharing
GQA with 10 heads and 5 KV heads, XSA on last 2 blocks

Within each loop iteration

Late Training Replay: 2-epoch replay of last 100 training batches at 10% learning rate
Self-distillation with EMA teacher, 50 steps, temperature=2.0, alpha=0.7

novel_contributions: Recursive weight sharing architecture applying K unique transformer blocks N times to create deeper effective networks with fewer parameters
Asymmetric weight sharing (Micro Crawler) isolating gradient conflict to fewer blocks to improve quality and quantization robustness
Bidirectional persistent deliberation gate enabling communication between recursive firings with gradient flow in both directions
Reinvestment of saved parameter budget into wider 4x MLP layers enabled by fractal weight sharing
Demonstration that steps matter more than depth due to faster training steps with recursive sharing

title: Add_Maestro_Solar_Protocol_Joeavaib
author: Joeavaib

data.description: Specialized layer stack with reasoning, completion, and validation layers including SolarShield gating and LeakyReLU(0.5)^2 activation

parameters.reasoning_layer: L1 with sharper attention temp=0.7 and plan vector generation
parameters.validation_layer: L9 with softer attention temp=1.3 and SolarShield gating
parameters.SolarShield_gating: reality-locked gating at L0/L4 balancing residual stream flow

data.description: LeakyReLU(0.5)^2 activation replacing standard relu² or SiLU to maintain gradient flow with non-negative inductive bias

novel_contributions: Maestro OS framework with 1+7+1 layer architecture specialized for reasoning, completion, and validation
SolarShield gating mechanism dynamically balancing residual stream flow
Use of LeakyReLU(0.5)^2 activation in MLP layers
Integration of score-first test-time training (TTT) protocol
Combination of Parallel Muon optimizer with EMA and Tight SWA weight averaging
Quantization using GPTQ-lite int6 with LZMA compression

title: Record: Full GPTQ + LeakyReLU² + Parallel Muon (3-seed mean 1.1180)
author: kshitizz36

data.description: Use of squared LeakyReLU activation function
Optimizer technique named Parallel Muon
BigramHash with parameters (3072,80)

novel_contributions: Full GPTQ quantization applied
Use of LeakyReLU squared activation function
Parallel Muon optimizer technique
BigramHash architectural modification with parameters (3072,80)
Independent 3-seed evaluation with sliding window stride=64
No test-time training (TTT) used

title: Non-record: 11L GEPA + 20k Steps + Pure Int6 + Legal TTT (val_bpb=1.0983): unlimited compute: 4×A100-40GB, ~2.8 hours
author: Christopher-Lee-McClendon
quantization: int6 per-row quantization with GPTQ-lite clip search
optimizer: Muon (matrix LR) and Adam (scalar LR) for training; SGD with momentum for TTT

data.method: int6 per-row with GPTQ-lite clip search

data.description: Cross-sequence attention on last 4 layers
Learned token-mixing gate on input embeddings
2048 buckets, 128-dim embeddings
Partial RoPE with 16/64 dims and YARN scaling
3× expansion with ReLU² activation
128d on layers 9–10 with per-layer scale initialized at 0.1
LayerNorm scale with 1/sqrt(layer+1) depth scaling
Residual connections across layer pairs
Weight tying of embeddings
Legal score-first test-time training with sliding windows and SGD momentum

novel_contributions: Extended training to 20000 steps with 8000-step cosine warmdown, showing warmdown length as dominant lever for float base improvement
Pure int6 per-row quantization with 15-candidate GPTQ-lite clip search and zstd-22 compression achieving smallest artifact size (14.22 MB)
Legal score-first test-time training (TTT) using SGD with momentum and learning rate warmup, achieving −0.044 BPP gain
Demonstrated that longer warmdown phase produces significant float base BPP decline (from ~1.216 to 1.1153)
Combined multiple architecture modifications including XSA on last 4 layers, SmearGate, BigramHash embeddings, Partial RoPE, and ReLU² MLP
